ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 covers oolong tea as a category; no specific entry exists for Lishan high-mountain oolong drip bag format. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers oolong tea generically; no specific code for Lishan high-altitude or drip-bag format. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers oolong tea as a substance; no specific concept for Lishan origin or drip-bag preparation. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: Lishan high-mountain oolong tea leaves (Camellia sinensis)

serving.description: 1 drip tea bag brewed in 200 ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One drip tea bag (4 g dry leaf) brewed in approximately 200 ml of hot water, yielding one cup of brewed oolong tea.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (Oolong)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrients are reported per 100 g of brewed oolong tea (USDA SR Legacy item 171917). Lishan oolong is a high-mountain Taiwanese cultivar; brewed nutrient profile is consistent with generic brewed oolong tea. Dry-leaf macros are effectively zero in the brewed beverage at normal dilution. Values reflect the prepared beverage as consumed.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Plain Brewed Tea — analogous estimate based on negligible carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed oolong tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates (~0.15 g per 100 ml). No specific GI test exists for Lishan oolong drip tea. By analogy with plain brewed tea (effectively zero glycemic response), GI is set to 1 (lowest valid positive value per convention) and gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per 200 ml serving is 0.0 given carbohydrate_g rounds to 0.3 g per serving, below threshold for meaningful GL. Consistent with University of Sydney GI Database guidance that unsweetened teas have negligible GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~3 mg per 100 ml brewed); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened brewed Lishan oolong tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension; its negligible sodium content (~6 mg per 200 ml cup) poses no meaningful blood-pressure risk, and regular tea consumption has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in observational studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and sugar content; no glycemic impact when consumed unsweetened
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened Lishan oolong tea is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it contains effectively zero digestible carbohydrates per serving, produces no meaningful glycemic response, and polyphenols in oolong tea have been studied for potential insulin-sensitising effects in small clinical trials.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols (catechins, theaflavins)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unsweetened Lishan oolong tea is cardioprotective in profile; it contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, and its polyphenol content may support endothelial function and LDL oxidation resistance, consistent with cardiovascular benefit observed in prospective cohort studies of regular tea drinkers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lishan Oolong Drip Tea Bag Gift Set (10 x 4g) is a premium Taiwanese high-mountain oolong tea product sold by CHICHA San Chen in Singapore. The tea leaves are hand-picked from Lishan (梨山), Taichung County, Taiwan, at altitudes of 2,000–2,200 metres above sea level during the Spring and Winter harvest seasons. At this elevation, cooler temperatures and misty conditions slow leaf growth, concentrating aromatic compounds and producing a tea with a complex floral-fruity fragrance, a smooth buttery mouthfeel, and a lingering sweet aftertaste characteristic of high-mountain oolongs. The gift set contains 10 individually packaged drip-bag (挂耳) sachets, each holding 4 g of whole or lightly rolled oolong tea leaves. The drip-bag format suspends the sachet over a cup, allowing hot water to pass through the leaves without requiring a teapot or strainer, making it convenient for office or travel use. CHICHA San Chen offers two roasting levels — lighter roast (floral, green-oolong character) and medium-to-darker roast (toasty, caramel notes) — catering to different palate preferences. Nutritionally, brewed Lishan oolong tea is essentially calorie-free (~1 kcal per 100 ml), contains no fat, no sugar, and negligible carbohydrates, making it suitable for individuals managing weight, blood glucose, or cardiovascular risk. It is naturally caffeine-containing (approximately 30–50 mg per 200 ml cup), which should be noted for caffeine-sensitive individuals. The polyphenol content, including EGCG and other catechins, contributes antioxidant properties. This product is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is positioned as a gift item at SGD 49.50 for the 10-bag set.
common_user_queries: Is oolong tea good for diabetes?
How many calories in oolong tea?
What is a drip tea bag?
Is Lishan oolong tea high in caffeine?
Is oolong tea suitable for hypertension?
CHICHA San Chen tea gift set calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed oolong tea (FDC 171917), which is the closest approved-source match for a generic brewed oolong tea. No specific laboratory analysis exists for Lishan high-mountain oolong from this vendor. The brewed beverage nutrient profile is highly consistent across oolong tea varieties at normal brewing dilution, justifying medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct GI test exists for this product.
notes: 1. Nutrients are reported per 100 g of brewed oolong tea as consumed (1 drip bag / 4 g dry leaf brewed in 200 ml water). Dry-leaf macros are not reported here as the product is consumed as a brewed beverage. 2. protein_g is naturally zero in brewed oolong tea at normal dilution; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 171917. 3. fat_g is naturally zero in brewed oolong tea; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 171917. 4. sugar_g is naturally zero as no sugars are added and tea polysaccharides do not contribute measurable free sugars to the brewed liquor. 5. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero in the brewed beverage (fibre remains in the leaf, which is not consumed). 6. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Glycemic load per 200 ml serving is 0.0. 7. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for brewed oolong tea. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for brewed oolong tea. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for brewed oolong tea. 10. This product is sold as a gift set (10 x 4 g drip bags) at SGD 49.50 by CHICHA San Chen at One Fullerton, Singapore, via field survey. 11. Caffeine content is approximately 30–50 mg per 200 ml brewed cup (not included in per_100g as caffeine is not a standard nutrient field in this template). 12. The cuisine is classified as Chinese (Taiwanese origin); multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given the food is sold in Singapore.

improvement_actions_taken: Set glycemic_index to 1 (not null or 0) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g, fat_g, sugar_g, and dietary_fibre_g.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-gap reasons in notes.
Used only 'closest_parent' or 'exact' for all ontology match_type fields; no invented values.
Applied Singaporean dish multilingual rule (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given product is sold in Singapore.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent before finalising.
